Fix: tabs block scrollbar caused by overflow-x-auto without overflow-y-hidden.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Gate now checks .bot_directives before approving actions

Adds Check 0 (runs first) to the Gate evaluation pipeline: reads
.bot_directives from the target repo to determine if the requesting
bot is allowed to perform the action.

Directive format:
- .bot_directives/<bot_id>.json — per-bot config
- .machine_readable/bot_directives.json — unified config
- .bot_directives/default.json — fallback

Supports:
- blocked_bots: list of bot IDs that cannot touch this repo
- allowed_actions: per-bot list of permitted action types

Open by default — repos without directives allow all bots.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add full Forced Mobility system — the core intelligence model

PHILOSOPHY:
Demand alone is not enough. The strongest signal is:
  people_released × transport_weakness × time_pressure × ride_quality

This models the difference between ACTIVITY and RIDE CONVERSION.

NEW FILE: src/lib/flow-engine/forced-mobility.ts

TYPES:
- ForcedMobilityCategory: 8 categories
  - station_release, airport_release, event_exit
  - nightlife_closure, banlieue_return_constraint
  - office_release, transport_disruption, compound

- RideProfile: short_fast | mixed | long | premium_long

- ForcedMobilityWave: full signal schema with 4-dimension scoring

SCORING SYSTEM (0-100 each dimension):

1. people_released_score (weight: 0.35)
   - Estimated count + release concentration (instant/waves/gradual)

2. transport_weakness_score (weight: 0.30)
   - 01:00-05:30 = 100 (no metro)
   - 23:00-01:00 = 60-90 (last metros)
   - Boosted by: strikes, disruptions, rain

3. time_pressure_score (weight: 0.20)
   - Late night = 95, rush hour = 70-75, daytime = 30
   - Weekend modifier

4. ride_quality_score (weight: 0.15)
   - Luggage, international, premium district
   - Distance expectation, competition level

COMPOUND BOOST: 1.3x when 2+ factors overlap

WAVE BUILDERS:
- buildStationReleaseWave() - trains + metro weakness
- buildEventExitWave() - events + transit weakness
- buildNightlifeClosureWave() - clubs + no metro

PRIORITY ORDER:
1. compound (100)
2. airport_release (90)
3. station_release (80)
4. banlieue_return_constraint (75)
5. event_exit (70)
6. nightlife_closure (60)
7. transport_disruption (55)
8. office_release (40)

SNCF INTEGRATION:
- fetchStationForcedMobilityWaves() - builds FM waves from arrivals
- forcedMobilityWaveToSignal() - bridge to existing signal model

EXAMPLE OUTPUT:
  Gare du Nord at 00:45 with Eurostar + metro closed:
  - people_released: 70
  - transport_weakness: 90
  - time_pressure: 95
  - ride_quality: 85
  - final_score: 82 (compound)
  - label: "GARE + METRO FAIBLE"
  - profile: "Courses longues garanties"

This is the system that makes Flow feel magical.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Show save error inline next to title, remove reserved space

Replace the fixed-height block below the title with an inline flex row.
Only the error state renders (no Saving…/Saved ✓), so there's no layout
shift and no empty space when idle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(parity): wounded legs check before kick direction + legs_in_no_shape fixes

C's dokick() checks Wounded_legs (line 1279) BEFORE getdir() (line 1318).
JS's handleKick asked "In what direction?" immediately, skipping the
wound check. This caused key stream desynchronization: C consumed a
--More-- dismiss key while JS consumed a direction key.

Fix: add wound check + --More-- to handleKick before direction prompt.

Also fixes legs_in_no_shape():
- BOTH_SIDES/LEFT_SIDE/RIGHT_SIDE constants were 0x10/0x20/0x30 but
  actual values from const.js are 0x20000/0x40000/0x60000
- EWounded_legs called without player argument
- Same constant fix in set_wounded_legs() and heal_legs()

seed033 divergence: step 48 → step 84 → step 104.

commit.message:
Complete remaining Catan theory bot improvements across all decision systems

Multi-action winning turns:
- checkWinningMove now chains road building → settle, year of plenty → build,
  monopoly for winning resources, and road building for longest road finisher
- Covers all "finish with a bang" combos (dev card + build in one turn)

Pre-roll knight optimization:
- Always play pre-roll when robber blocks high-value hex (unblock before production)
- Stronger army pursuit/defense probabilities
- Leader targeting: block their best hex before they collect
- Endgame: always play knights (VP + robber control)

VP card timing:
- If VP cards alone win the game, don't waste other dev cards
- Only play a knight alongside VP cards if it gives army bonus

Setup improvements:
- Road points toward resource complementarity (fills production gaps)
- Opponent proximity penalty 2.5x stronger (was -10/-3, now -25/-8)
- Critical pair penalty: -40 if NEITHER brick+lumber after both settlements
- Wool coverage bonus increased to 15

Strategy pivoting:
- Forced pivot when expansion is blocked (no brick/lumber or at max settlements)
- Fallback chain: expansion → cities → development based on actual production

Dev card sequencing:
- Road building prioritizes "road → settle same turn" combos
- Only plays road building toward good destinations (vertexScore > 5)
- Year of Plenty gates on build completion
- Monopoly disrupts opponent builds

Robber improvements:
- Target leader's hex when ANY opponent is within 2 VP of winning

commit.message:
fix: OneDNN OFF on non-BF16 ARM64 + Hetzner CAX41 INT8 benchmarks ($0.33/genome)

autoconfig.sh: Force TF_ENABLE_ONEDNN_OPTS=0 on Neoverse-N1/N2 and any
CPU without BF16. ACL FP32 GEMM adds 29% make_examples overhead vs Eigen
on N1 (332s vs 258s). Added safety net: if BF16=false && OneDNN=1, force
OneDNN=0 with warning.

Hetzner CAX41 benchmarks (16 shared vCPU, Neoverse N1, $0.043/hr):
- INT8 ONNX + jemalloc: 578s wall → $0.33/genome (15.2x cheaper than x86)
- TF Eigen FP32 + jemalloc: 735s wall → $0.42/genome
- Accuracy: SNP F1=0.9978, INDEL F1=0.9963 (PASS)
- Shared vCPU: ~6% CV variance, ~15% slower than dedicated Oracle A1

commit.message:
Stagger closer arrivals: each subsequent closer starts 30min later

Instead of all closers arriving at the same time, staggers arrivals
as demand ramps up. First closer arrives at template start, each
subsequent one 30 min later, capped so every shift meets minimum
hours. All closers still end at the same time (close).

Example for 5 closers on a Saturday (template 7:30 PM - 2:00 AM):
- Closer 1: 7:30 PM (first in, sets up section)
- Closer 2: 8:00 PM (demand building)
- Closer 3+: 8:00 PM (capped at 6h minimum shift)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add circuit breaker, request/SSE timeouts, readiness probe, Dockerfile, and E2E tests

- Circuit breaker (3-state: closed/open/half-open) with nil-receiver safety
- Request timeout via ResponseHeaderTimeout, context-aware body reader using io.Pipe + context.AfterFunc
- SSE idle timeout with goroutine lifecycle management
- Readiness probe (/readyz) with atomic cache, TryLock thundering-herd prevention, and isolated HTTP client
- Graceful shutdown coordination between main and management servers
- Config validation, transport/circuit-breaker config fields, Dockerfile
- Comprehensive E2E tests (1700+ lines) and unit tests for all new features
